response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with ~15 years of experience, strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD), and genuine systems-level exposure through DLang low-latency microservices and event-driven architectures. However, the role centers on hands-on C++/STL development for industrial automation, and his direct C++ experience is limited to a short 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments and a thesis project. There is no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL practices, industrial protocols, or Azure, and his recent decade has been predominantly web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js). The core skills gap is too large for a senior C++ role despite his overall seniority.
strengths: Deep experience with low-latency, event-driven, high-throughput systems (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
Broad polyglot background spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with concurrency and async I/O experience
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~6 months in 2014–2015); recent work is TypeScript/Node.js-focused
No demonstrated industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL) or industrial protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA)
No Azure or automation-machinery/IoT domain background; career trajectory points away from the C++ systems domain

key_factors.factor: No industrial security or protocol expertise
